---
title: "Performance Benchmarking fastats"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Performance Benchmarking fastats}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Introduction

Because `fastats` computes statistics using zero-allocation single-pass inline C++ loops, it circumvents R’s garbage collector (GC) completely during calculation phases. This vignette benchmarks `fastats` against standard `t.test()` operations using `bench`.

## Benchmarking Setup

```{r data-generation}
library(fastats)

if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {
  set.seed(42)
  x_large <- rnorm(1e6, mean = 5, sd = 2)
  y_large <- rnorm(1e6, mean = 4, sd = 1)
  
  results_welch <- bench::mark(
    Base_R = t.test(x_large, y_large, var.equal = FALSE),
    fastats = welch_t_test(x_large, y_large, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided"),
    check = FALSE,
    iterations = 100
  )
  
  print(results_welch[, c("expression", "min", "median", "mem_alloc", "n_gc")])
}
```
